title: Build Reliable AI Tutorials (Enhanced)
description: An enhanced specification demonstrating content style, gamification, adaptive paths, and SOTA educational features.

learningObjectives.description: Define quality gates for generated educational content.
Map tutorial claims to cited sources and verification states.

lessons.title: Quality Foundations
lessons.summary: Learn how explicit schemas and source rules reduce ambiguity in generated tutorials.

accessibility.altText: Flowchart showing spec to prompt to validation to review pipeline
accessibility.fallbackText: Tutorial specs feed structured prompts, then validation gates, then human review.

recommendations.rationale: Visualizes the quality gate pipeline for immediate comprehension.
recommendations.contentBrief: Flow diagram showing spec → prompt → validate → review pipeline.
recommendations.accessibilityNotes: Provide fallback text describing the four-stage pipeline.

metacognition.prompt: Before starting, rate your confidence in defining what makes educational content reliable.

lessons.title: Evidence Mapping
lessons.summary: Classify and trace tutorial claims back to their source evidence.

concepts.definition: A deterministic check that generated content must pass before publication.
A label indicating whether a factual statement is verified, needs verification, or is unsupported.

misconceptions.misconception: A model-generated lesson is ready when it sounds polished.
misconceptions.correction: Generated content still needs schema validation, source checks, accessibility checks, and human review.

workedExamples.title: Marking a claim for verification
workedExamples.prompt: A lesson says a tool is the most popular option but has no source.
steps: Add it to the source plan.
workedExamples.explanation: Popularity claims are unstable and need recent evidence.

exercises.prompt: List three checks that should run before publishing a generated lesson.

answers.answer: Schema validation, MDX compilation, citation checks, accessibility checks, and unsupported-claim detection.
rubric: Includes at least one source or claim check

formativeAssessment.prompt: Why should validation run before human review?
formativeAssessment.expectedAnswer: It catches structural and mechanical defects before reviewers spend time on pedagogy and factuality.

formativeAssessment.prompt: How should an unsupported claim be represented in MDX?
formativeAssessment.expectedAnswer: It should be wrapped in a verification state and connected to a source plan item.

masteryCriteria.description: Can explain the purpose of each required quality gate.
masteryCriteria.threshold: Accurately explains at least four gates.

masteryCriteria.description: Can classify claims as verified, verify, or unsupported.
masteryCriteria.threshold: Correctly classifies four out of five sample claims.

recap: Use structured specs to constrain generation.
Treat citations and verification states as required content, not polish.
nextSteps: Author a source plan for a real tutorial.
Run pnpm validate before requesting review.

culturalAssumptions: Examples assume familiarity with command-line development.

pointRules.description: Bonus points for perfect quiz score.

achievements.title: Advanced Unlocked
achievements.description: Completing the foundation lesson unlocks advanced content.

adaptivePaths.title: Deep Dive
adaptivePaths.description: Full path with all lessons and exercises.
adaptivePaths.entryCondition: Learner self-identifies as intermediate or advanced.

projectCapstones.title: Tutorial Quality Audit
projectCapstones.description: Audit an existing tutorial for quality gate compliance.
rubric: Identifies at least three quality gate failures
